CREATE TABLE data (y FLOAT, x FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE more_data (y FLOAT, x FLOAT);
SELECT regr_intercept(d.y, d.x) FROM data d JOIN more_data m ON d.x = m.x;
CREATE TABLE test_data (y FLOAT, x FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (NULL, 1.0), (1.0, NULL), (2.0, 2.0), (3.0, 3.0), (4.0, 4.0), (NULL, NULL);
SELECT regr_intercept(y, x) FROM (SELECT y, x FROM data WHERE y IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_data;
SELECT d.x, regr_intercept(y, x) OVER (PARTITION BY d.x) FROM data d;
SELECT regr_intercept(y, x) FROM (SELECT AVG(y) AS y, AVG(x) AS x FROM data) AS averages;
SELECT regr_intercept(y, x) FROM (SELECT y, x FROM data UNION ALL SELECT y, x FROM more_data) AS combined;
